title: Since vs For
shortSummary: Since is for a starting point; for is for a duration.
quickAnswer: Use 'since' with a specific point in time (e.g., Monday, 2020). Use 'for' with a period of time (e.g., two hours, five years).
coreDifference: Since indicates the start of a period; for indicates the length of a period.
englishExplanation: Since and for are used to talk about time, especially with perfect tenses. 'Since' tells us when something started. It is followed by a specific time or event, like 'since 2010' or 'since I was a child'. 'For' tells us how long something lasts. It is followed by a duration, like 'for three years' or 'for a long time'.

wordDetails.simpleMeaning: from a specific time in the past until now

examples.sentence: She has been sleeping since 10 p.m.
I have lived here for eight years.
We have known each other since we were kids.
They have been married for 20 years.

quiz.question: I have known her ___ 2018.
quiz.explanation: 2018 is a specific year, a starting point.

quiz.question: He has been a teacher ___ ten years.
They have been married ___ last June.
quiz.explanation: Last June is a specific point in time.

quiz.question: I haven't eaten ___ breakfast.
quiz.explanation: Breakfast is a specific meal time, a starting point.

seo.title: Since vs For: Difference, Examples, and Quiz | ClearWord Lab
seo.description: Learn the difference between since and for with simple explanations, natural examples, and practice questions.
